The prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam said in the narration of also in he said he said people are precious metals. The best of them in Jahyah are still the best of them after the coming of Islam as long as they have knowledge and knowledge here being referred to as understanding right it's not just facts it is true understanding and insight of the book of Allah of the sunnah of the prophet Sam. And so there is no way that a person can reach the heights of virtue without knowledge. No matter how good their intention is. As a matter of fact, the idea that one's intention can be very very good without knowledge is also incorrect because intention has details to it. Intention needs understanding and knowledge in order to purify it in order that a person may perfect his intention. And so all of these things need knowledge. There's no way to climb the rungs of the ladder of emen without knowledge. It is not possible. It's just you do not have the technology that you need in order to obtain that highness. No matter how much a person wants something, no matter how good their intention may be, no matter how strongly they desire it, if they don't have the means to do it, they will not be able to do it. And so the prophet sallall.aii wasallam places this as a condition. We see the next narration here. As a matter of fact, place this narration on the forefront of your mind. And whenever you look to do something or say something or make a decision, remember this statement. He says indeed the life of this duny this world is sweet and green. It's very alluring. It's very seductive. At first taste, it's very sweet. At first glance, it's very green. And this is not necessarily a bad thing. It is a bad thing when it is used for bad. And so we see a good woman will have a very good effect on her husband. A companion her dowy. summary: This lecture discusses Chapter 6 from Riyadul Saliheen, focusing on the essential role of Islamic knowledge in attaining virtue and closeness to Allah. The speaker explains that good intentions alone are insufficient without proper understanding of the Quran and Sunnah. The lecture then presents a hadith warning against the seductions of worldly life, particularly emphasizing the danger of romantic attraction to women. The speaker clarifies that this warning encompasses not just physical temptation but any romantic involvement that could lead a person away from their faith and obligations to Allah. He illustrates with examples of how women can be either a positive or negative influence on men's spiritual journey.
suggested_title: Knowledge as Foundation for Virtue and Warning Against Worldly Seductions - Chapter 6 of Riyadul Saliheen
